text: This article tries to follow the origins of the traditional or orthodox Trinitarian formula. The main aim of this article is to investigate to what extent the traditional Christian doctrine of the Trinity (Niceense formula) has been influenced by Greek philosophy. By way of introduction to the contemporary theological discussion on this issue, the article carries out a comparison between the traditional formulation of the doctrine of the Trinity as it has been influenced by Greek philosophy and the biblical revelation of the godhead. The conclusion reached is that the trinitarian formula was perhaps not a dogmatic doctrine, but a mystery (a dehellenisation of the concept of Trinity). The Church Fathers and the post-Nicene Church used the Greek philosophical-theological expressions of the Trinity, which had already been present in Scripture in its doxological and liturgical form primarily for the purpose of contextualisation.
